_meta.source: Joseph Myers, "Tiling with Regular Star Polygons", Eureka 56 (2004), pp. 20-27
_meta.transcribedBy: CC (Fable 5 session), 2026-07-10, from the PDF figure captions fetched from polyomino.org.uk. Captions quoted verbatim in myersCaption; tokens use the same convention as myers-2009-k2.json: angle unit = pi/12 (15 deg); n*p@u = star point of angle u units; n*d@u = star dent of interior u units (u = 24 - 24/n - alphaU).
_meta.caution: Myers' enumeration is a hand enumeration; he found 3 tilings missing from Gruenbaum-Shephard (Figs 2a, 4l, 4n), and the k=2 sequel carries an explicit not-double-checked caveat. Recovering this list is machine cross-verification of an unchecked enumeration, not validation against proven ground truth.
_meta.conventions: Vertices are points where >= 3 tiles meet; a dent filled entirely by one corner is NOT a vertex (Myers p.21). Figure 2 = not edge-to-edge (out of engine scope); Figure 3 = edge-to-edge, some dent at a vertex (four entries are infinite alpha-families); Figure 4 = edge-to-edge, no dent at a vertex.
_meta.engineExpectations: The star24 engine palette also contains the regular octagon, so the engine additionally enumerates the 11 pure-regular uniform tilings over {3,4,6,8,12} (Myers Fig 1; includes 4.8.8 = t1002) - those are matched against knownRegular below, not against the star records. In-ring members of the Fig-3 alpha-families at palette alpha values are expected engine output and matched against the family templates.

records.notes: 9-fold, needs D=36; one of Myers' 3 additions over G&S
